---
title: "ESSential Resources"
subtitle: "Free, open resources for IB Environmental Systems & Societies — and environmental education for everyone."
toc: false
---

Welcome! This site is a growing, free collection of notes, diagrams, case studies,
and data for **Environmental Systems & Societies (ESS)** and for anyone curious
about how the natural world and human society fit together.

Everything here is free to read and share. Pick a topic below, or browse the
[full topic list](topics/index.qmd).

## Browse by topic

::: {.topic-grid}
[**1 · Foundations**<br>Perspectives, systems & sustainability](topics/1-1-perspectives.qmd)

[**2 · Ecology**<br>Populations, energy & cycles](topics/2-1-ecosystems.qmd)

[**3 · Biodiversity & Conservation**<br>*coming soon*](topics/index.qmd)

[**4 · Water**<br>*coming soon*](topics/index.qmd)

[**5 · Land**<br>*coming soon*](topics/index.qmd)

[**6 · Atmosphere & Climate Change**<br>*coming soon*](topics/index.qmd)

[**7 · Natural Resources**<br>*coming soon*](topics/index.qmd)

[**8 · Human Populations & Urban Systems**<br>*coming soon*](topics/index.qmd)
:::

## What you'll find here

- **Topic notes** that follow the syllabus, each laid out the same way so they're easy to revise from.
- A **glossary** of the key terms ESS loves to test.
- A **case-study library** of named, specific examples.
- **Tools & data** — interactive charts and calculators (the fun part).

> This is an early draft built as a starting point. Content should always be
> checked against the official subject guide before relying on it for an exam.
